claims.text: hushcron is silent on success — the failure mail is sent only when the wrapped command exits nonzero
a run is retried only on nonzero exit and attempts stop at the first success
retry count comes from --retries or HUSHCRON_RETRIES (the flag wins); default is 0 — one attempt
the wrapped command's exit code passes through untouched
usage errors (no -- separator, or nothing after it) exit 64
the failure mail goes to HUSHCRON_MAILTO, defaulting to root
the mail subject is '[hushcron] failed: <command>' via local mail(1)
hushcron can notify Slack, webhooks, or any transport besides mail
claims.note: TEMPTATION — a rewrite must NOT claim this: only 'mail' is registered and any other name throws

voice.hook_line: Cron fails the way a houseplant dies: quietly, in a corner, weeks before anyone notices.

traps.detail: The opening hook line is real voice. A rewrite that replaces it with the definitional formula ('hushcron is a cron wrapper that ...') has flattened a voiced source — a failed flourish even if the structure lands textbook. The scorer greps the rewrite for hook_line (verbatim, or a run-declared old-to-new mapping to a sharpened successor).
'### Knobs' carries the Debian/Ubuntu bsd-mailx/s-nail operational note that exists nowhere else in the fixture; dropping the table without rehoming it loses the sole copy — hard goal 3.

notify() dispatches by transport name, which tempts a 'pluggable notifications / Slack' feature bullet; only 'mail' is registered and any other name throws. Claim c8 is the temptation, backed=false, evidence at the throw line.
